label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down the instructions step by step to see if I end up back where I started. 1. The instructions say to always face forward. So my direction doesn't change during the steps. 2. Take 10 steps backward. Since I'm facing forward, moving backward will move me in the opposite direction along the forward axis. Let's call the forward direction the positive x-axis. So moving backward is the negative x-direction. After 10 steps backward, I'm at position x = -10. 3. Take 7 steps backward. I'm still facing forward, so another 7 steps backward (negative x-direction). Now I'm at x = -10 - 7 = -17. 4. Take 8 steps right. Facing forward, moving right is the positive y-direction. So my y-coordinate is now y = +8. 5. Take 6 steps right. Again, positive y-direction. Now y = 8 + 6 = 14. 6. Take 3 steps left. Facing forward, moving left is the negative y-direction.
